filesModified.purpose: Added useSelect to fetch category accent_color term meta via getEntityRecord('taxonomy', 'category', id), applied the color class to the pill <span> using cn() with computed property key
Added get_term_meta() call to fetch category accent_color, applied the color class to the pill <span> using class_name() with dynamic key

warnings: Asset size warnings for editor.js and screen bundles (pre-existing)

syncStatus.details: Both TSX and PHP apply the category accent color class conditionally on the pill span. TSX uses cn({ [categoryAccentColor]: !!categoryAccentColor }), PHP uses class_name([ $categoryAccentColor => !empty($categoryAccentColor) ]). Same base classes, same conditional logic.

fixesApplied.description: Changed topic tag pill to use the category's accent_color term meta instead of inheriting the page/section accent color. The color class (e.g., 'blue', 'green', 'purple') is applied directly on the pill <span> element, which scopes the --accent-color CSS custom properties so that bg-accent-lighter and border-accent resolve to the category-specific color.
fixesApplied.approach: Applied the same pattern used by card-post.php in the Archive block: get the category's accent_color term meta and apply it as a CSS class on the element. In TSX, added a separate useSelect hook to fetch the category entity record and extract meta.accent_color. In PHP, used get_term_meta($firstCategory->term_id, 'accent_color', true). The color class overrides --accent-color, --accent-color-light, --accent-color-lighter CSS custom properties within the element scope.
